Several years ago, I developed a PRNG that runs on mental arithmetic and generates sequences of digits. Here's how it works: 

Start with a seed between 1-99 (I usually use seconds on my watch, but with no electronics, just pick one). Then...

text: Multiply the ones place of the seed by 7, and the tens place by 3, and add those products together. The ones place of the sum is the output digit, and the full sum is the new seed for generating more digits.

raw.full_text: Multiply the ones place of the seed by 7, and the tens place by 3, and add those products together. The ones place of the sum is the output digit, and the full sum is the new seed for generating more digits.

text: I forget the exact criteria I used to pick 3 and 7 as the multipliers. I think it was something like "maximize the length of the shortest seed cycle".
